ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Subject group as defined in the study
Notes: bn= Bulimia Nervosa; hc= Healthy Control

ElementDescription: The fasted and fed-state of subject
Notes: fasted= fasted state, no standardized meal before MRI scan; fed= fed state, ate the standardized meal before MRI scan

ElementDescription: Task phase
Working memory load, ''n-back'' indicates how many letters are between repetitions of the same letter (i.e., targets)
Round number
Notes: Each level of the n-back is repeated twice (i.e., for 2 rounds)

ElementDescription: Trial number (Phase 1)
Stimulus Type
Participant response to the trial
Stimulus onset time
Reaction time to each trial
Accuracy of each trial
Trial number (Phase 2/3)
Choice onset time (relative to fMRI trigger time)
Response time for each trial (relative to choice onset time)
Participant's choice to either perform a low-effort task for a small monetary reward or a high-effort task for a larger reward
Notes: 1= Low effort task; 2= High effort task

ElementDescription: Monetary reward offered for the lower-effort task
Monetary reward offered for the higher-effort task
n-back level of the high-effort task
n-back level of the low-effort task
How close the offer for the easy task is to the participant's indifference point for that hard task
Notes: Smaller absolute value of proximity setting means a closer proximity to the indifference point
